dataset: Synthetic PRL data generated via dual-rate Rescorla-Wagner simulation (random.seed(2024))

pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh (lines 86-103: PRL-999 generated with random.random() < 0.5 choices ignoring feedback) and confirmed by live testing inside VM: PRL-999 accuracy=0.485 (near chance), only 3 reversals vs 6-17 for valid participants, and single-rate model fit produces beta=0.5 (minimum grid value) indicating degenerate/unidentifiable parameters.

Verified by live testing: successfully wrote JSON to this path as user ga, confirmed directory exists with ga:ga ownership and 755 permissions, and export_result.sh checks this exact path for anti-gaming timestamps.

Verified by live testing: file exists at 55,435 bytes, owned by ga:ga with 644 permissions. Contains 3001 lines (1 header + 3000 data rows = 15 participants x 200 trials). Columns confirmed: participant_id, trial, choice, outcome, rewarded_stimulus. MD5 hash 587c881e51bb00d66a3f5e25dc3293bc verified deterministic across regeneration.

Rescorla-Wagner model confirmed published in 1972: 'A theory of Pavlovian conditioning: Variations in the effectiveness of reinforcement and nonreinforcement' by Rescorla & Wagner, in Classical Conditioning II (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rescorla%E2%80%93Wagner_model). AIC introduced by Hirotugu Akaike, first presented 1971, published 1973: 'Information theory and an extension of the maximum likelihood principle' (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akaike_information_criterion). The task formula AIC = 2k - 2*log_likelihood matches the standard formulation.

privileged_info_summary: The data file prl_model_data.csv contains 3000 rows (15 participants x 200 trials) with columns participant_id, trial, choice, outcome, rewarded_stimulus. Data is synthetic, generated with random.seed(2024) using a dual-rate Rescorla-Wagner model with 80/20 probabilistic rewards and 8-of-10-correct reversal criterion. PRL-999 is the contaminated participant who chose randomly (50/50) regardless of feedback; its accuracy is 0.485, it completed only 3 reversals, and its single-rate model fit yields beta=0.5 (minimum grid value) with the worst log-likelihood (-139.87), confirming degenerate/unidentifiable parameters. The 14 valid participants have the following ground-truth single-rate fits: PRL-001 alpha=0.25 beta=5.5 LL=-88.40 AIC=180.79; PRL-002 alpha=0.45 beta=9.0 LL=-53.51 AIC=111.02; PRL-003 alpha=0.10 beta=2.0 LL=-127.55 AIC=259.10; PRL-004 alpha=0.60 beta=4.5 LL=-67.16 AIC=138.31; PRL-005 alpha=0.20 beta=6.0 LL=-87.08 AIC=178.15; PRL-006 alpha=0.35 beta=18.0 LL=-27.34 AIC=58.67; PRL-007 alpha=0.25 beta=7.5 LL=-73.23 AIC=150.45; PRL-008 alpha=0.10 beta=3.0 LL=-123.88 AIC=251.76; PRL-009 alpha=0.45 beta=10.0 LL=-40.65 AIC=85.31; PRL-010 alpha=0.10 beta=4.5 LL=-117.53 AIC=239.06; PRL-011 alpha=0.35 beta=18.5 LL=-29.29 AIC=62.57; PRL-012 alpha=0.35 beta=1.0 LL=-131.59 AIC=267.18; PRL-013 alpha=0.35 beta=5.0 LL=-78.94 AIC=161.89; PRL-014 alpha=0.45 beta=7.0 LL=-67.41 AIC=138.81. Ground-truth dual-rate fits: PRL-001 ap=0.35 an=0.15 beta=6.5 LL=-86.98 AIC=179.96; PRL-002 ap=0.40 an=0.45 beta=9.5 LL=-53.48 AIC=112.96; PRL-003 ap=0.10 an=0.10 beta=2.0 LL=-127.55 AIC=261.10; PRL-004 ap=0.70 an=0.55 beta=4.5 LL=-66.96 AIC=139.93; PRL-005 ap=0.25 an=0.20 beta=5.5 LL=-86.93 AIC=179.87; PRL-006 ap=0.30 an=0.35 beta=19.5 LL=-27.15 AIC=60.29; PRL-007 ap=0.40 an=0.20 beta=7.0 LL=-70.64 AIC=147.27; PRL-008 ap=0.05 an=0.20 beta=4.0 LL=-123.10 AIC=252.19; PRL-009 ap=0.40 an=0.45 beta=10.5 LL=-40.62 AIC=87.24; PRL-010 ap=0.05 an=0.15 beta=6.0 LL=-117.15 AIC=240.30; PRL-011 ap=0.65 an=0.30 beta=16.5 LL=-25.69 AIC=57.39; PRL-012 ap=0.50 an=0.05 beta=4.0 LL=-128.96 AIC=263.93; PRL-013 ap=0.50 an=0.20 beta=6.0 LL=-77.45 AIC=160.90; PRL-014 ap=0.35 an=0.50 beta=8.0 LL=-66.92 AIC=139.85. AIC model comparison results: 9 participants prefer single_rate (PRL-002, PRL-003, PRL-004, PRL-005, PRL-006, PRL-008, PRL-009, PRL-010, PRL-014) and 5 prefer dual_rate (PRL-001, PRL-007, PRL-011, PRL-012, PRL-013). Group summary: mean_alpha=0.3107, mean_beta=7.25, n_prefer_single_rate=9, n_prefer_dual_rate=5. Per-participant accuracy: PRL-001=0.590, PRL-002=0.505, PRL-003=0.560, PRL-004=0.720, PRL-005=0.575, PRL-006=0.510, PRL-007=0.610, PRL-008=0.550, PRL-009=0.685, PRL-010=0.560, PRL-011=0.575, PRL-012=0.580, PRL-013=0.605, PRL-014=0.680, PRL-999=0.485. Per-participant reversals: PRL-001=13, PRL-002=11, PRL-003=6, PRL-004=17, PRL-005=13, PRL-006=12, PRL-007=13, PRL-008=8, PRL-009=17, PRL-010=8, PRL-011=14, PRL-012=7, PRL-013=14, PRL-014=16, PRL-999=3.
